provenance.origin: Original controlled English benchmark authored for this repository on 2026-07-07.
provenance.method: Manual construction from standard lexical-ambiguity, attachment-ambiguity, garden-path, and anaphora construction families; no model output was used to generate, filter, rank, or label items.
provenance.canonical_examples: Garden-path structures are original lexical variants of standard psycholinguistic construction types; no published item list was copied.
provenance.license_note: Distributed with this repository under the repository's terms.

design.id_contract: IDs are stable, category-prefixed, zero-padded identifiers and must never be recycled.
design.deduplication: Sentences are unique after Unicode NFKC normalization, whitespace collapse, and case-folding.
design.concept_label_contract: Each reading has a distinct, lowercase semantic proxy intended to be one Qwen next-token unit after a leading-space boundary. Exact single-token status is a runtime gate against the pinned tokenizer revision; a failed label must reject the item rather than be silently split.
design.garden_path_reading_contract: For reduced-relative and NP/Z items, r1 is the globally licensed reanalysis and r2 is the locally licensed initial parse that creates the garden path; r2 need not integrate the disambiguating spillover. Gerund-participle items instead retain two globally licensed parses.

tokenizer_validation.concept_method: Accept the first one-token surface among a leading-space concept label and the bare label.
tokenizer_validation.probe_method: Render the full probe, append one space plus A or B, require exact prompt-prefix preservation, and require exactly one appended token.

behavior_probe_contract.token_boundary: The scored continuation surface is one space followed by the ASCII token label after the final colon; validate prompt-prefix preservation and exactly one appended token for the pinned Qwen tokenizer.
behavior_probe_contract.counterbalancing: Run a mirrored probe with reading-to-letter assignments reversed. Report the mean signed margin after orienting both probes to target reading r1; retain both raw margins.
behavior_probe_contract.interpretation: Target and foil define a signed comparison axis, not a gold intended meaning. The clean model's commitment is whichever oriented reading has the larger counterbalanced logit.

items.sentence: The bank closed before sunset.

readings.description: A financial institution stopped operating for the day.
The land beside a river became closed or inaccessible.

items.sentence: The bat landed near the fence.

readings.description: A flying mammal came down near the fence.
A piece of sports equipment fell near the fence.

items.sentence: The crane moved slowly across the yard.

readings.description: A long-legged bird walked across the yard.
A lifting machine traveled or swung across the yard.

items.sentence: The seal was difficult to replace.

readings.description: A trained marine animal was difficult to substitute.
A closure, gasket, or stamped seal was difficult to replace.

items.sentence: Everyone noticed the spring.

readings.description: Everyone noticed the season following winter.
Everyone noticed a source of water emerging from the ground.

items.sentence: The match ended abruptly.

readings.description: A sporting contest ended abruptly.
A small fire-lighting stick burned out or was extinguished abruptly.

items.sentence: The bark was unusually rough.

readings.description: The outer covering of a tree had a rough texture.
A dog's vocal sound was unusually harsh.

items.sentence: The club was left in the basement.

readings.description: A social organization was abandoned or housed in the basement.
A heavy striking weapon was left in the basement.

items.sentence: They searched for the mole all night.

readings.description: They searched for a small burrowing animal.
They searched for a covert insider or spy.

items.sentence: The jam lasted for hours.

readings.description: A traffic blockage lasted for hours.
An informal music session lasted for hours.

items.sentence: The draft caused several complaints.

readings.description: A preliminary version of a document caused complaints.
Compulsory military recruitment caused complaints.

items.sentence: They called the package light.

readings.description: They said the package had little weight.
They said the package had a pale color.

behavior_probe.question: In what sense is the package light?

items.sentence: The plane was on the workbench.

readings.description: A model or small aircraft was on the workbench.
A woodworking tool for smoothing surfaces was on the workbench.

items.sentence: The pitcher broke during practice.

readings.description: The baseball player was physically or emotionally broken during practice.
A handled liquid container shattered during practice.

items.sentence: The file was missing from the cabinet.

readings.description: A folder or stored document was missing.
A metal shaping tool was missing.

items.sentence: His watch lasted four hours.

readings.description: His timepiece functioned for four hours before stopping.
His assigned period of guard duty continued for four hours.

items.sentence: The palm was clearly visible.

readings.description: A palm tree was clearly visible.
The inner surface of a hand was clearly visible.

foil.choice: the inner surface of a hand

items.sentence: The pupil appeared unusually large.

readings.description: A school student appeared unusually large in body size.
The dark opening in an eye was unusually dilated.

items.sentence: The organ was restored last year.

readings.description: A large keyboard instrument was restored.
A biological body part was restored or transplanted.

items.sentence: The bolt struck without warning.

readings.description: A flash of lightning struck without warning.
A metal fastener or projectile struck something without warning.

items.sentence: The scale looked shiny.

readings.description: A weighing instrument had a shiny surface.
A small plate from a fish or reptile looked shiny.

foil.choice: a plate on a fish or reptile

items.sentence: The current was stronger than expected.

readings.description: The movement of water was stronger than expected.
The flow of electricity was stronger than expected.

items.sentence: The date was surprisingly sweet.

readings.description: A romantic outing was emotionally pleasant.
A date fruit had a sugary taste.

items.sentence: The old port was famous.

readings.description: An old harbor town or facility was famous.
An aged fortified wine was famous.

items.sentence: The glasses were left on the counter.

readings.description: Eyeglasses were left on the counter.
Drinking vessels were left on the counter.

items.sentence: The cap was removed after the inspection.

readings.description: A hat or protective cover was physically removed.
An upper limit or restriction was abolished.

items.sentence: The court was crowded that morning.

readings.description: A legal tribunal or courtroom was crowded.
A playing surface for a sport was crowded.

items.sentence: The pen was beside the shed.

readings.description: A writing instrument was beside the shed.
An enclosure for animals was beside the shed.

items.sentence: They found the mine near the border.

readings.description: They found an excavation for extracting minerals.
They found a concealed explosive device.

items.sentence: The mouse stopped moving.

readings.description: A small rodent stopped moving.
A computer pointing device stopped tracking movement.

items.sentence: I saw the guard with the binoculars.

readings.description: I used binoculars to see the guard.
I saw a guard who possessed binoculars.

behavior_probe.question: How does with the binoculars attach?

target.choice: the binoculars were used for seeing

items.sentence: The photographer captured the tourist with the camera.

readings.description: The photographer used the camera to take the picture.
The photographed tourist was carrying the camera.

behavior_probe.question: Who is associated with the camera?

items.sentence: Sara struck the intruder with the umbrella.

readings.description: Sara used the umbrella as the instrument of striking.
Sara struck an intruder who was holding the umbrella.

behavior_probe.question: How does with the umbrella attach?

target.choice: Sara used the umbrella to strike

items.sentence: The detective followed the suspect in the blue car.

readings.description: The detective was in the blue car while following.
The suspect being followed was in the blue car.

behavior_probe.question: Who was in the blue car?

items.sentence: I watched the girl on the balcony.

readings.description: I was on the balcony while watching the girl.
The girl I watched was on the balcony.

items.sentence: I heard the musician in the hallway.

readings.description: I was in the hallway when I heard the musician.
The musician I heard was in the hallway.

items.sentence: The spy observed the courier near the fountain.

readings.description: The spy was near the fountain while observing.
The observed courier was near the fountain.

items.sentence: The officer stopped the driver with the red flag.

readings.description: The officer used a red flag as a signal to stop the driver.
The officer stopped a driver who possessed a red flag.

target.choice: the officer used it as a signal

items.sentence: Maya greeted the guest in the silk robe.

readings.description: Maya was wearing the silk robe when she greeted the guest.
The guest whom Maya greeted was wearing the silk robe.

items.sentence: Riley called the doctor from the office.

readings.description: Riley made the call while Riley was in the office.
Riley called a doctor who was at or associated with the office.

items.sentence: Pat emailed the client from Paris.

readings.description: Pat sent the email while Pat was in Paris.
The client whom Pat emailed was from Paris.

behavior_probe.question: Who was in or from Paris?

items.sentence: Jordan spoke to the assistant in the laboratory.

readings.description: Jordan was in the laboratory while speaking.
The assistant Jordan spoke to was in the laboratory.

items.sentence: Robin waved to the neighbor on the balcony.

readings.description: Robin was on the balcony while waving.
The neighbor receiving the wave was on the balcony.

items.sentence: Leslie interviewed the witness from the balcony.

readings.description: Leslie conducted the interview while Leslie was on the balcony.
The witness being interviewed was on the balcony.

items.sentence: Morgan discussed the friend at the airport.

readings.description: Morgan held the discussion while Morgan was at the airport.
The discussion concerned a friend who was at the airport.

behavior_probe.question: How does at the airport attach?

foil.choice: it identifies the friend at the airport

items.sentence: The reporter described the attack on television.

readings.description: The reporter's description was broadcast on television.
The attack being described occurred or appeared on television.

items.sentence: The company announced the merger in April.

readings.description: The announcement was made in April.
The merger was scheduled or occurred in April.

items.sentence: The captain reported the accident on Tuesday.

readings.description: The captain made the report on Tuesday.
The accident happened on Tuesday.

items.sentence: The committee delayed the vote on Monday.

readings.description: The act of delaying occurred on Monday.
The vote that was delayed had been scheduled for Monday.

items.sentence: The scientist reviewed the forecast for June.

readings.description: The review was intended or scheduled for June.
The forecast concerned conditions expected in June.

target.choice: it dates or motivates the review

items.sentence: She remembered the lecture in Boston.

readings.description: She was in Boston when she remembered the lecture.
The lecture she remembered had taken place in Boston.

items.sentence: The editor reviewed the letter from Rome.

readings.description: The editor reviewed the letter while working from Rome.

target.choice: it locates the editor during the review

items.sentence: The memo about the meeting on Friday was brief.

readings.description: The memo was issued, written, or relevant on Friday.
The meeting discussed in the memo was scheduled for Friday.

items.sentence: The note about the package on the desk disappeared.

readings.description: The note was on the desk before it disappeared.
The package mentioned by the note was on the desk.

items.sentence: The photograph of the children by the fountain was torn.

readings.description: The physical photograph was located by the fountain.
The children depicted in the photograph were by the fountain.

items.sentence: The painting of the ship in the gallery was stolen.

readings.description: The painting was displayed in the gallery.
The painting depicted a ship located in a gallery.

items.sentence: The daughter of the nurse with curly hair arrived.

readings.description: The daughter had curly hair.
The nurse had curly hair.

items.sentence: The assistant to the professor with the award resigned.

readings.description: The assistant had received or possessed the award.
The professor had received or possessed the award.

items.sentence: The neighbor of the singer with the dog waved.

readings.description: The neighbor was accompanied by or owned the dog.
The singer was accompanied by or owned the dog.

items.sentence: The friend of the baker from Madrid called.

readings.description: The friend was from Madrid.
The baker was from Madrid.

items.sentence: The mare raced past the stable stumbled near the gate.

readings.description: The mare that someone raced past the stable later stumbled.
The mare itself raced past the stable, the initially preferred main-verb analysis.

behavior_probe.question: What role does the mare have in raced?

target.choice: the mare was raced by someone

items.sentence: The defendant examined by the solicitor appeared uneasy.

readings.description: The defendant was examined by the solicitor and appeared uneasy.
The defendant examined someone, the temporary main-verb analysis before by forces revision.

behavior_probe.question: What role does the defendant have in examined?

items.sentence: The soldiers warned about the avalanche conducted the rescue.

readings.description: The soldiers who had been warned about the avalanche conducted the rescue.
The soldiers warned other people about the avalanche, the temporary active analysis.

behavior_probe.question: What role do the soldiers have in warned?

items.sentence: The patient sent to the clinic arrived after noon.

readings.description: The patient was sent to the clinic and later arrived.
The patient sent something to the clinic, the temporary main-verb analysis.

behavior_probe.question: What role does the patient have in sent?

items.sentence: The suspect questioned in the corridor confessed immediately.

readings.description: The suspect was questioned in the corridor and then confessed.
The suspect questioned another person, the temporary main-verb analysis.

behavior_probe.question: What role does the suspect have in questioned?

items.sentence: The dog walked around the park collapsed at home.

readings.description: The dog that someone walked around the park later collapsed.
The dog walked itself around the park, the temporary main-verb analysis.

behavior_probe.question: What role does the dog have in walked?

foil.choice: the dog walked on its own

items.sentence: The messenger sent to the office vanished overnight.

readings.description: The messenger was sent to the office and later vanished.
The messenger sent something to the office, the temporary active analysis.

behavior_probe.question: What role does the messenger have in sent?

items.sentence: The student taught by the tutor passed easily.

readings.description: The student was taught by the tutor and passed.

behavior_probe.question: What role does the student have in taught?

items.sentence: The child dressed in the bedroom complained about the costume.

readings.description: Someone dressed the child in the bedroom; that child later complained.

items.sentence: The actor photographed on the stage smiled at the crowd.

readings.description: The actor was photographed on the stage and then smiled.

items.sentence: While the seamstress dressed the baby played in the crib.

readings.description: The seamstress dressed themself; the baby was the subject of played.
The seamstress dressed the baby, the initially preferred object analysis.

behavior_probe.question: What grammatical role does the baby have?

items.sentence: While the ranger hunted the deer ran into the clearing.

readings.description: The ranger hunted unspecified prey; the deer was the subject of ran.
The ranger hunted the deer, the initially preferred object analysis.

behavior_probe.question: What grammatical role does the deer have?

items.sentence: As the chef cooked the onions burned on the stove.

readings.description: The chef cooked something else; the onions were the subject of burned.
The chef cooked the onions, the initially preferred object analysis.

behavior_probe.question: What grammatical role do the onions have?

items.sentence: While the parents watched the child sang in the choir.

readings.description: The parents watched generally; the child was the subject of sang.
The parents watched the child, the initially preferred object analysis.

behavior_probe.question: What grammatical role does the child have?

items.sentence: Before the guard questioned the prisoner escaped through the window.

readings.description: The guard questioned someone else; the prisoner was the subject of escaped.
The guard questioned the prisoner, the initially preferred object analysis.

behavior_probe.question: What grammatical role does the prisoner have?

items.sentence: When the instructor lectured the student yawned loudly.

readings.description: The instructor lectured generally; the student was the subject of yawned.

items.sentence: When the doctor examined the patient sneezed twice.
